name: Villacarrillo from Villanueva del Arzobispo — circular through the olive groves of the Villas
name_original: Villacarrillo desde Villanueva del Arzobispo — circular por los olivares de las Villas
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Villanueva Del Arzobispo are set within the diverse landscapes of Jaén, Andalusia. The region is characterized by the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, featuring pine forests, elevated hills, and deep gorges. Additionally, the area includes the Vía Verde del Aceite, a reconditioned old railway line that traverses extensive olive groves and impressive metallic viaducts. This combination offers varied cycling experiences, from challenging mountain routes to accessible, largely flat paths. Best touring cycling routes around Villanueva Del Arzobispo The most popular touring cycling route is Villacarrillo from Villanueva del Arzobispo — circular through the olive groves of the Villas, a 21.4 miles (34.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete, winding through the region's characteristic olive groves. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Transandalus: Beas de Segura Loop, a moderate 10.9 miles (17.5 km) path. This route offers a scenic loop through the local landscape, often completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes. Local touring cyclists also love the Tranco de Beas Reservoir – Tranco de Beas Reservoir loop from Mirador del Control Viejo, a 44.1 miles (71.0 km) trail leading through the area around the Tranco de Beas Reservoir, often completed in about 5 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Villanueva Del Arzobispo is defined by mountainous terrain, extensive olive groves, and repurposed railway infrastructure like the Vía Verde del Aceite. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Villanueva Del Arzobispo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 118 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanueva Del Arzobispo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Villanueva Del Arzobispo?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Villanueva Del Arzobispo, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in this region?
A: The terrain around Villanueva Del Arzobispo is quite varied. You'll find challenging mountainous routes within the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, characterized by pine forests, elevated hills, and deep gorges. In contrast, the Vía Verde del Aceite offers largely flat and accessible paths through extensive olive groves, featuring impressive metallic viaducts and old railway tunnels.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: While many routes in the mountainous areas are considered difficult, there are moderate options available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996260192/transandalus-beas-de-segura-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transandalus: Beas de Segura Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 17.5 km path that offers a scenic experience. The Vía Verde del Aceite, though not specifically listed in our top routes, is generally known for being a largely flat and accessible route, suitable for various cycling abilities and families.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see along the cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and points of interest. You can encounter stunning mountain scenery within the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. Along some routes, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3168204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Aceite&lt;/a&gt; or the vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranco de Beas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Cabeza de la Viña Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirabueno Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offer panoramic views of the landscape.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995672434/villacarrillo-from-villanueva-del-arzobispo-circular-through-the-olive-groves-of-the-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villacarrillo from Villanueva del Arzobispo — circular through the olive groves of the Villas&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through characteristic olive groves. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24524127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranco de Beas Reservoir – Tranco de Beas Reservoir loop from Mirador del Control Viejo&lt;/a&gt;, a longer route around the reservoir.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling around Villanueva Del Arzobispo?
A: The region generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are often ideal, with milder temperatures and pleasant weather for exploring both the mountainous areas and the Vía Verde del Aceite. Summers can be very hot, especially in the plains, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if cycling during this season.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about cycling in Villanueva Del Arzobispo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from the challenging climbs and stunning views of the Sierra de Cazorla to the unique experience of cycling through olive groves on repurposed railway lines like the Vía Verde del Aceite. The variety of routes catering to different skill levels is also a key highlight.

Q: Are there any specific routes that showcase the region's olive groves?
A: Absolutely. The region is famous for its olive groves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995672434/villacarrillo-from-villanueva-del-arzobispo-circular-through-the-olive-groves-of-the-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villacarrillo from Villanueva del Arzobispo — circular through the olive groves of the Villas&lt;/a&gt; route is specifically designed to take you through these characteristic landscapes. Additionally, the Vía Verde del Aceite, a reconditioned old railway line, is renowned for traversing extensive olive groves, offering a unique cultural and scenic experience.

Q: Can I find routes with significant elevation gain for a more challenging ride?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a challenge, the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park provides routes with significant elevation gain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38154097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranco de Beas Reservoir – Segura de la Sierra loop from Mirador del Control Viejo&lt;/a&gt; features nearly 2000 meters of ascent, making it a demanding ride for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by water bodies or offer opportunities for a swim?
A: Yes, some routes will take you near water. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40016078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Aceite - Swim Spot loop from Villanueva del Arzobispo&lt;/a&gt; is a route that specifically highlights a swim spot. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24524127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranco de Beas Reservoir – Tranco de Beas Reservoir loop from Mirador del Control Viejo&lt;/a&gt; also circles a large reservoir, offering scenic views of the water.

Q: Is public transport available to access cycling routes, or is parking readily available?
A: While specific public transport links directly to trailheads can be limited in rural areas, Villanueva del Arzobispo itself has bus connections. For those traveling by car, parking is generally available in and around the town, especially for tourists. When planning a route, it's advisable to check specific starting points for designated parking areas, particularly near popular natural parks or attractions.

Q: Are there any multi-day cycling options or longer routes in the vicinity?
A: Yes, for those seeking more extensive cycling adventures, the GR247, also known as the "Sendero de Bosques del Sur," is a spectacular circular route of over 300 km that traverses the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. This multi-day bikepacking route offers stunning landscapes and connects with traditional villages, appealing to those looking for a longer journey.
tips: Even in the hilly areas there are olive plantations everywhere. Very nice way. Can be easily ridden with touring bikes.

Spanish town and municipality located in the southwestern part of the Las Villas region, in the province of Jaén.
The municipality of Villacarrillense includes the population centers of Villacarrillo, which holds the capital of the region of Las Villas, Mogón, La Caleruela, Agrupación de Mogón and Arroturas.

waypoints.name: Church of the Assumption of Villacarrillo
